exam.title: Web Development Summative Exam
exam.description: Interactive summative assessment covering architecture, RESTful APIs, Laravel, Nuxt, frontend/backend concepts, Git workflow, and deployment-related fundamentals.

questions.question: Which statement best describes a RESTful API?
choices: An API that always returns HTML pages for browsers
An API that organizes resources through endpoints and commonly uses HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
An API that only works with Laravel applications
An API that requires server-side rendering to function
questions.correctAnswer: An API that organizes resources through endpoints and commonly uses HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
questions.explanation: RESTful APIs usually expose resources through URLs and standard HTTP methods.

questions.question: Match each architectural part with its main responsibility.
rightItems: Handles request flow and delegates work

correctPairs.Controller: Handles request flow and delegates work

questions.explanation: This separation helps keep code organized and maintainable.

questions.question: Which are benefits of using a well-structured architecture such as MVC with services and repositories?
questions.explanation: Architecture improves structure and growth potential, but it does not magically remove all bugs.

questions.question: What is scaffolding in web development?
choices: A process for deleting unused code automatically
A way to generate initial boilerplate code and project structure
questions.correctAnswer: A way to generate initial boilerplate code and project structure
questions.explanation: Scaffolding speeds up development by generating common files and structure.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of using software design patterns?
choices: To make every project use identical UI
To solve common design problems using reusable approaches
To remove the need for testing
questions.correctAnswer: To solve common design problems using reusable approaches
questions.explanation: Patterns provide proven approaches to recurring design problems.

questions.question: Why are components commonly used in frontend frameworks?
choices: To repeat the same code everywhere
To break the UI into reusable, manageable parts
questions.correctAnswer: To break the UI into reusable, manageable parts
questions.explanation: Components improve reuse, readability, and maintainability of UI code.

questions.question: What is the role of a layout in a web application?
choices: It defines the repeated page structure shared across views
It serves as the database schema
questions.correctAnswer: It defines the repeated page structure shared across views
questions.explanation: Layouts often include shared headers, footers, navigation, and wrappers.

questions.question: Laravel is primarily a:
questions.correctAnswer: PHP web framework
questions.explanation: Laravel is a PHP framework commonly used for backend development.

questions.question: Categorize each item correctly.
questions.explanation: Laravel and Nuxt are frameworks; PHP, JS, TS, and HTML are languages or markup.

questions.question: Why are semantic HTML elements important?
choices: They improve meaning, accessibility, and structure
questions.correctAnswer: They improve meaning, accessibility, and structure
questions.explanation: Semantic tags help browsers, developers, and assistive technologies understand content.

questions.question: Match each semantic HTML tag with its common purpose.
questions.explanation: Semantic tags describe content roles more clearly than generic containers.

questions.question: What does separation of concerns promote?
choices: Putting all logic in one file
questions.correctAnswer: Dividing responsibilities into focused parts
questions.explanation: Separation of concerns makes systems easier to debug, update, and scale.

questions.question: What is a CDN commonly used for?
choices: Serving static assets from distributed locations
questions.correctAnswer: Serving static assets from distributed locations
questions.explanation: CDNs help deliver static files efficiently across regions.

questions.question: In general, routing determines:
choices: Which content or handler is shown for a specific URL
questions.correctAnswer: Which content or handler is shown for a specific URL
questions.explanation: Routing maps paths to handlers, pages, or components.

questions.question: A frontend app needs to fetch all users from a backend API. Complete the request definition.
questions.explanation: A common REST-style request for fetching data uses GET and often expects JSON.

questions.question: Which of the following are frameworks covered in class?
questions.explanation: Laravel and Nuxt are frameworks; HTML is markup and GitHub is a platform.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a pull request?
choices: To run seeders automatically in production
To propose and review code changes before merging
questions.correctAnswer: To propose and review code changes before merging
questions.explanation: Pull requests support review, discussion, and safer integration.

questions.question: What issue is shown in this snippet?
questions.snippet:
<<<<<<< HEAD
const title = 'Exam A';
=======
const title = 'Exam B';
>>>>>>> feature-branch
questions.explanation: The markers indicate Git could not automatically combine two competing changes.

questions.question: Match each tool or concept to its purpose.
rightItems: Online repository hosting and collaboration platform

correctPairs.GitHub: Online repository hosting and collaboration platform

questions.explanation: These are core version control and collaboration concepts.

questions.question: Categorize each responsibility as frontend or backend.
questions.explanation: Frontend focuses on user-facing rendering; backend handles data, logic, and APIs.

questions.question: Match each server or terminal role with its purpose during local development.
rightItems: Serves UI and client-side app during development
Runs API logic and processes requests

correctPairs.Backend server: Runs API logic and processes requests
correctPairs.Frontend server: Serves UI and client-side app during development

questions.explanation: Projects often run separate local processes for frontend, backend, and database.

questions.question: Match each database-related term with its role.
rightItems: Populates tables with initial or test data

correctPairs.Seeder: Populates tables with initial or test data

questions.explanation: Migrations shape the schema; seeders fill the data.

questions.question: What is the typical purpose of a .env file?
questions.correctAnswer: To store environment-specific configuration values
questions.explanation: The .env file usually stores configurable values such as database credentials and app settings.

questions.question: In a PHP project, composer.json is mainly used to:
questions.correctAnswer: Declare project dependencies and scripts
questions.explanation: Composer uses composer.json to manage PHP packages and project configuration.

questions.question: What architectural issue is most visible in this controller code?
choices: The controller contains too much business logic and responsibilities
The controller should return HTML instead of JSON
questions.correctAnswer: The controller contains too much business logic and responsibilities
questions.explanation: Controllers should stay focused on request handling and delegate business logic to services.

questions.question: In MVC, a model commonly represents:
questions.correctAnswer: Application data and related behavior
questions.explanation: Models often map to data entities and may define relationships.

questions.question: Why is a JSON response commonly used in APIs?
choices: Because browsers cannot read anything else
Because it is a lightweight and widely used data format for client-server communication
Because it replaces all database queries
questions.correctAnswer: Because it is a lightweight and widely used data format for client-server communication
questions.explanation: JSON is a common format for exchanging structured data in APIs.

questions.question: An endpoint is best described as:
choices: A specific URL where an API resource or action can be accessed
questions.correctAnswer: A specific URL where an API resource or action can be accessed
questions.explanation: Endpoints identify where requests are sent in an API.

questions.question: Complete the Authorization header format used for bearer token authentication.
questions.explanation: Bearer is the common authentication scheme used in Authorization headers.

questions.question: What is middleware commonly used for in a backend framework?
choices: Intercepting requests for tasks like authentication, logging, or filtering
questions.correctAnswer: Intercepting requests for tasks like authentication, logging, or filtering
questions.explanation: Middleware sits in the request pipeline and can allow, modify, or block requests.

questions.question: What is Postman mainly used for?
choices: Testing and inspecting API requests and responses
questions.correctAnswer: Testing and inspecting API requests and responses
questions.explanation: Postman is a popular API development and testing tool.

questions.question: Match each HTTP status code with its meaning.
questions.explanation: These are common status codes used in APIs.

questions.question: What describes server-side rendering (SSR)?
choices: Rendering the page content on the server before sending it to the client
Running Git commands in the browser
questions.correctAnswer: Rendering the page content on the server before sending it to the client
questions.explanation: SSR can improve first-page load and SEO in many cases.

questions.question: What does a base API URL usually represent?
choices: The root address used before specific endpoints
questions.correctAnswer: The root address used before specific endpoints
questions.explanation: For example, a client may use a base URL and append endpoints like /users or /posts.

questions.question: Which statements about headers and application/json are correct?
choices: Headers can carry metadata about a request or response
Headers are only used for CSS
Authorization may also be sent in headers
correctAnswers: Headers can carry metadata about a request or response
Authorization may also be sent in headers
questions.explanation: Headers describe request and response details such as content type and auth information.

questions.topic: Business Logic -> Service / Database Access -> Repository
questions.question: Place each responsibility in the correct layer.
items: Compute final discount based on rules
Fetch user by email from database
questions.explanation: Services handle business rules; repositories handle persistence and data access.

questions.question: Frontend routing usually controls:
choices: How pages or components change in the client application without full page reloads
questions.correctAnswer: How pages or components change in the client application without full page reloads
questions.explanation: Client-side routing is a core concept in SPA and modern frontend frameworks.

questions.question: Why is proper error handling important in applications and APIs?
choices: To provide clear feedback and avoid unpredictable failures
To remove the need for status codes
questions.correctAnswer: To provide clear feedback and avoid unpredictable failures
questions.explanation: Good error handling improves reliability, debugging, and user experience.

questions.question: Arrange these Laravel setup-related steps in a reasonable development sequence.
questions.explanation: The exact workflow can vary, but this is a sensible setup order.

questions.question: Arrange these Nuxt setup-related steps in a reasonable development sequence.
items: Open the app in the browser
correctOrder: Open the app in the browser
questions.explanation: This reflects a common local setup flow for Nuxt projects.

questions.topic: Personal Access token table and relation to laravel middleware
questions.question: A protected Laravel API route should only be accessible when the client sends a valid bearer token. Which combination best explains the role of personal access tokens and middleware?
choices: The token table stores CSS variables and middleware renders layouts
Personal access tokens can be stored and checked, while middleware protects routes by validating incoming authenticated requests
Middleware creates database tables and the token table generates HTML forms
The token table replaces controllers and middleware replaces repositories
questions.correctAnswer: Personal access tokens can be stored and checked, while middleware protects routes by validating incoming authenticated requests
questions.explanation: Token records support authentication, while middleware enforces access control on routes.

questions.topic: Multiple use of repository in service files
questions.question: A service needs to combine data from UserRepository and OrderRepository to decide whether a customer qualifies for a reward. What does this best demonstrate?
choices: A service can coordinate multiple repositories to implement business logic
A repository should directly render the frontend page
A model must always replace repositories
questions.correctAnswer: A service can coordinate multiple repositories to implement business logic
questions.explanation: Services often orchestrate multiple repositories when business rules depend on several data sources.

questions.topic: Architecture / Separation of Concerns / MVC / Service / Repository
questions.question: Arrange the common request handling flow for a layered backend architecture.
questions.explanation: A common pattern is request -> controller -> service -> repository -> database -> response.

questions.question: Complete the request needed to create a new post in a protected API that expects JSON.
questions.explanation: Protected JSON APIs commonly require POST, Content-Type: application/json, and Authorization: Bearer <token>.

questions.question: A client successfully creates a new resource through an API endpoint. Which response is most appropriate?
questions.correctAnswer: Status 201 with a JSON response
questions.explanation: A successful creation commonly returns 201 and often includes a JSON representation.

questions.question: Arrange this common collaboration workflow in a reasonable order.
questions.explanation: This reflects a common Git collaboration process.

questions.question: Which issue is most clearly shown in this HTML?
choices: Too much semantic structure is used
It uses only generic elements instead of clearer semantic tags
It is invalid because div cannot be nested
questions.correctAnswer: It uses only generic elements instead of clearer semantic tags
questions.explanation: Semantic tags like header, nav, and main can communicate structure more clearly.

questions.question: Categorize each item correctly.
questions.explanation: Some files are project configuration, while others support database structure and data.

questions.question: What is the likely result if this route is protected by auth middleware but the request has no bearer token?
choices: The request should be rejected as unauthorized
The request will become a POST request automatically
The middleware will insert a token
The route will return HTML instead of JSON
questions.correctAnswer: The request should be rejected as unauthorized
questions.explanation: Protected routes commonly require valid authentication credentials.

questions.question: Why are interfaces useful in an architecture with repositories?
choices: They define contracts so implementations can be swapped more easily
They prevent the use of middleware
questions.correctAnswer: They define contracts so implementations can be swapped more easily
questions.explanation: Interfaces improve flexibility, consistency, and testability.

questions.question: A project has repeated business rules copied across several controllers. Which change most directly improves maintainability?
choices: Move repeated logic into a service layer
Add more inline SQL in controllers
questions.correctAnswer: Move repeated logic into a service layer
questions.explanation: Centralizing business logic reduces duplication and eases updates.

questions.question: Which design choice most supports scalability as an application grows?
choices: Keeping database, UI, and business rules all in one file
Using separated layers with clear responsibilities
questions.correctAnswer: Using separated layers with clear responsibilities
questions.explanation: Clear structure helps teams extend features and manage complexity.

questions.question: Nuxt is primarily used for:
choices: Building applications in the JavaScript ecosystem, commonly with Vue-based architecture
questions.correctAnswer: Building applications in the JavaScript ecosystem, commonly with Vue-based architecture
questions.explanation: Nuxt is used for frontend or full-stack web app development around Vue concepts.

questions.question: TypeScript is generally understood as:
choices: A typed superset used in the JavaScript ecosystem
questions.correctAnswer: A typed superset used in the JavaScript ecosystem
questions.explanation: TypeScript adds static typing features to JavaScript development.

questions.question: A frontend app has a shared navbar and footer across many pages, plus reusable cards on each route. Which approach is most appropriate?
choices: Use a layout for shared page structure and components for reusable UI parts
Put all pages into one giant HTML file without routing
Store UI elements inside the database
Move all frontend logic into backend repositories
questions.correctAnswer: Use a layout for shared page structure and components for reusable UI parts
questions.explanation: Layouts handle repeated page shells; components handle reusable pieces.

questions.question: A controller catches an exception during API processing. What is the most appropriate response behavior?
choices: Return a meaningful error response with an appropriate status code
Always return status 200 even when there is an error
Output raw stack traces to all users
Ignore the error and continue saving broken data
questions.correctAnswer: Return a meaningful error response with an appropriate status code
questions.explanation: APIs should communicate failures clearly and safely.

questions.question: You want to test a protected API endpoint manually before connecting the frontend. Which tool and setup is most appropriate?
choices: Use Postman and include the Authorization header
Use only HTML semantics without requests
Use GitHub Issues to execute the endpoint
Use composer.json as the request client
questions.correctAnswer: Use Postman and include the Authorization header
questions.explanation: Postman is commonly used to test endpoints with headers and payloads.

questions.topic: Laravel setup commands / composer.json / .env / Migration / Seeders
questions.question: A fresh Laravel project cannot connect to the database and the tables do not exist yet. Which combination of actions is most appropriate?
choices: Check .env database settings, install dependencies, and run migrations
Delete composer.json and only edit HTML
Create semantic tags and push directly to GitHub
Use frontend routing to generate tables
questions.correctAnswer: Check .env database settings, install dependencies, and run migrations
questions.explanation: Configuration, dependencies, and migrations are central to backend setup.

questions.question: If the base API URL is /api and the users endpoint is /users, the complete route becomes ________.
questions.explanation: The endpoint path is appended to the base API path.

questions.question: Case Study: You are building a web app with a Nuxt frontend and a Laravel backend. The app should use reusable components, page layouts, API endpoints that return JSON, route protection using bearer tokens, and clear separation of controller, service, and repository responsibilities. Answer the linked items.

subItems.question: Where should business logic primarily live?
Where should database access primarily live?
Which header is commonly used to send a token?
Which response format is commonly expected from the backend API?
Which frontend feature best supports shared page wrappers across screens?

questions.explanation: This case combines frontend structure, backend layering, API design, and authentication.
